title: For a Naughty Little Girl by Ann Taylor — Sad Poetry Lines
content:
My sweet little girl should be cheerful and mild
She must not be fretful and cry!
Oh! why is this passion? remember, my child,
GOD sees you, who lives in the sky.

That dear little face, that I like so to kiss,
How alter'd and sad it appears!
Do you think I can love you so naughty as this,
meaning: "For a Naughty Little Girl" by Ann Taylor is a sad and love poem consisting of 29 lines. The poet explores themes of sad and love through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "My sweet little girl should be cheerful and mild She must not be fretful and cry!...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Ann Taylor's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
